title_en: Laos has big dreams of exporting energy
title_zh: 老挝的能源出口梦想很大
rubric_en: But its dams have flooded it with debt and other problems
rubric_zh: 但它的水坝也让国家陷入债务和其他问题

paragraphs.en: WITH ITS large ambitions for data centres and factories, South-East Asia needs energy. Last month the International Energy Agency estimated that the region would account for a fifth of the increase in global power demand between now and 2035; only India is expected to contribute more. Yet as the war in Iran has painfully illustrated, secure supplies cannot be taken for granted. Where could the juice come from?
paragraphs.zh: 随着老挝对数据中心和工厂提出了宏大的发展计划，东南亚需要更多能源。上个月，国际能源署估计，该地区在截至2035年的全球用电需求增长中将贡献五分之一；只有印度预计会贡献更多。然而，伊朗战争已经痛苦地证明，稳定的能源供应绝不是理所当然的。能源的“电力来源”又该从哪里来？

paragraphs.en: Laos believes that it could be part of the answer. Coming from officials belonging to a landlocked country of around 8m people and an income per person of only $2,400, that sounds audacious. But Laos is rich in one respect: water. The Mekong, South-East Asia’s longest river, and its tributaries tumble through the country’s mountains. Harnessing all that falling water offers an abundant source of clean energy. Laos wants to use it to become the “battery of South-East Asia”, as enthusiasts term it, and the core of a regional grid.
paragraphs.zh: 老挝认为它可以成为答案的一部分。这个人口约800万、全国人均收入只有2400美元的内陆国家的官员提出这一想法，听上去很大胆。但老挝在一个方面非常富有：水。东南亚最长的河流湄公河及其支流在这里的山区奔流而下。利用这些下泄的水量可以带来充足的清洁能源。老挝想要用这笔“水能”成为所谓的“东南亚电池”，并成为区域电网的核心。

paragraphs.en: The idea of such a South-East Asian network was first dreamt up as long ago as the late 1990s, though Laos had been exporting power long before that. It first sold hydro-generated electricity to Thailand, with which it shares a long border, in 1971. Since then it has built more than 80 hydro plants, with a total capacity of more than 12GW, enough to meet Singapore’s peak demand, and accounting for around 70% of Laos’s own supply. By 2030 it hopes to be operating 100 dams and to have lifted capacity above 15GW. Electricity is already the country’s biggest export. In 2024 Laos sold power to six countries, including Singapore (some 2,000km away), raking in $2.4bn, which was nearly a quarter of its total exports.
paragraphs.zh: 这种东南亚区域网络的构想早在1990年代末就已出现，尽管老挝早在那之前就开始出口电力。它最早于1971年向与之接壤很长边界的泰国出售水电。在此之后，它建成了80多座水电站，总装机容量超过12吉瓦，足以满足新加坡的峰值需求，并约占老挝自身电力供应的70%。到2030年，它希望能够运行100座水坝，并将装机容量提高到15吉瓦以上。电力已经是老挝最大的出口商品。2024年，老挝向六个国家出售电力，包括远在约2000公里外的新加坡，收入24亿美元，几乎占其全部出口的四分之一。

paragraphs.en: Much of this growth is due to China, which borders Laos to its north. This year a new transmission line linking the two countries opened up, allowing far bigger sales northwards. But Chinese influence extends well beyond electricity generation. The two countries, both nominally communist one-party states, have long shared an affinity. That has deepened thanks to a flood of Chinese investment in railways, rare-earth mines and dams. Around half of Laos’s hydropower projects are believed to involve Chinese financing. In 2021 Électricité du Laos (EDL), the state utility, handed a 90% stake in a transmission grid to a subsidiary of China Southern Power Grid, a state-owned firm, under a 25-year concession.
paragraphs.zh: 这一增长很大程度归功于中国，与老挝接壤于北面的中国。今年，一条连接两国的新输电线路通车，允许更大规模的向北输电销售。但中国的影响远不止发电领域。两国都是名义上的单一共产主义政党国家，这种关系长期以来就很亲近。随着中国在铁路、稀土矿和水坝上的大量投资，这种亲近进一步加深。据信，老挝大约一半的水电项目都涉及中国融资。2021年，老挝国家电力公司“老挝电力公司”（Électricité du Laos，简称EDL）将输电网的90%股份交给中国南方电网子公司，在25年特许经营期内。

paragraphs.en: But the China-fuelled dam-building frenzy has come at a cost. Public debt is estimated to have ballooned to roughly 81% of GDP in 2025, up from about 53% a decade ago. Around half of the country’s borrowing is owed to China, which has repeatedly staved off crises by deferring repayments. Many dams have been financed under build-operate-transfer deals, in which ownership will eventually revert to the Lao government. But such transfers will take time. According to the Lowy Institute, an Australian think-tank, the earliest Laos can expect to take over a big hydropower project is 2035. Environmentalists, meanwhile, blame the dams for collapsing fish stocks in the Mekong and the erosion of river banks along it.
paragraphs.zh: 但是，由中国推动的修坝热潮是有代价的。到2025年，公开估算显示公共债务可能已飙升至约占国内生产总值（GDP）的81%，而十年前约为53%。全国一半左右的借款都欠给了中国，而中国多次通过延迟还款来暂时化解危机。许多水电站是按“建设—运营—移交”模式融资的，最终所有权会回归老挝政府，但这种移交需要时间。根据澳大利亚智库洛维研究所（Lowy Institute）的说法，老挝最早能够接管一个大型水电项目的时间要到2035年。与此同时，环保主义者将湄公河鱼类资源崩塌和沿岸河岸侵蚀都归咎于这些大坝。

paragraphs.en: The quest for exports has also meant neglect at home. EDL is in the red because of transmission losses and other infrastructure problems. Power cuts remain common, especially in the dry season, when the Mekong runs low. This means that Laos has to often import electricity from Thailand at a higher price than it sells it for, according to Len George of the Asian Development Bank. To tackle this, the country is now investing more in solar power, which it also hopes to export. In April South-East Asia’s largest solar plant, of 1GW, opened in the country’s north. This too was built by a Chinese state firm to serve the Chinese market.
paragraphs.zh: 对外出口的追求也意味着国内被忽视。由于输电损耗和其他基础设施问题，老挝国家电力公司（EDL）一直处于亏损状态。尤其在旱季，当湄公河水量下降时，停电仍然很常见。这意味着，正如亚洲开发银行（ADB）亚洲发展银行工作人员伦·乔治（Len George）所说，老挝经常不得不以高于其对外售电价格的价格从泰国进口电力。为了解决这一问题，该国正加大对太阳能的投资，并希望将其也用于出口。4月，东南亚最大的1吉瓦（1GW）太阳能电站在老挝北部开工并投入运行。该项目同样由一家中国国有企业建设，服务的对象是中国市场。

paragraphs.en: Exporting energy may help Laos’s neighbours, but its economy has its own needs. Investment in power has crowded out spending on schools and clinics. Laotians stream across to Thailand in search of better-paid work. In November the country is due to graduate from the UN’s list of least-developed countries. To keep climbing, however, Laos will need more than the Mekong to charge its economy. ■
paragraphs.zh: 向外出口能源可能惠及老挝邻国，但老挝经济也有自身需求。电力投资挤占了对学校和诊所的支出。许多老挝人渡过边境到泰国去寻找更高薪的工作。11月，老挝预计将从联合国最不发达国家名单中毕业。要想继续向上发展，老挝却需要的不仅仅是湄公河来“给经济充电”。■

paragraphs.en: For exclusive coverage of Asian politics, economics and security, sign up to Asia Bulletin, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 若想获取亚洲政治、经济与安全领域的独家深度报道，请订阅《亚洲简报》（Asia Bulletin），这是我们每周仅对订阅用户开放的通讯。
